commit.message:
feat: gallery redesign, schedule simplification, and lazy image loading (#105)

- Gallery: full-width responsive tiles (height scales with panel width),
  overlaid action buttons (bottom-left/right), virtualized list that
  loads thumbnails on-demand and unloads them when scrolled off screen
- Thumbnails bumped to 600px wide for sharp display at full width
- Schedule UI: replaced 3-mode dropdown with Interval + Cron only;
  Interval mode adds a secondary type picker (Second–Year); both modes
  show a human-readable cron description; invalid cron shows field guide
- --debug flag now enables the Debug mode checkbox on launch
- Fixed InvalidCastException when NumericUpDown interval value is cleared
- Fixed NullReferenceException in OnTabSelectionChanged during XAML init
- Gallery only loads when the Gallery tab is selected (lazy tab load)

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fluid interface, search v2, AI chat v3, remove categories

- Remove categories system (table, keywords, classify, UI components)

- Add new migrations (019-030) for search improvements
- Update admin catalog to work without categories

- 4 trend following (SuperTrend, MTF EMA, momentum)
- 2 range expansion (Larry Williams, FVBO)

86% PnL from 1 trade. Not ready for inclusion.

Restore home tagline, move search into ranking pages, capitalize Ranking

- Home: restore H1 tagline + subtitle, hide ranking heading (RankingTable showHeading=false)
- Ranking subpages: H1 dynamic title with SearchBar below, no external SearchBar
- EN titles: capitalize "Ranking" (ES/BR already had it capitalized)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: mobile touch controls — add touch-action CSS and iOS gesture prevention

The touch event handlers were present but the browser's default touch
behaviors (scroll, pinch-zoom, double-tap zoom) were overriding them.
Added touch-action:none to body/canvas CSS, disabled user-scalable in
viewport meta, prevented iOS gesture events, and moved preventDefault()
before early returns so it always fires.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove conflicting /index.tsx so Sandpack's built-in bootstrap renders the app

When AI generates files as index.tsx, the toSandpackFiles function copies the
content to /App.tsx for the entry point, but was leaving the original /index.tsx
which overwrote Sandpack's built-in bootstrap (import App; render). Now we delete
the remapped /index.tsx so Sandpack's internal entry point works correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Hamburger moves to right (last child of nav, space-between → logo left)

- nav z-index raised to 1002 > mob-menu 1000 → hamburger always tappable to close
- Menu links: font-style italic (matches hero 'Ideas shaped with intent')

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Show layer names inline on Show click; keep frame selected

Selecting child nodes while a frame is selected doesn't produce

- Viewport still scrolls/zooms to the flagged nodes
- Clicking Show toggles a layer name list directly in the
  suggestion card (↳ LayerName rows, up to 10)
- nodeNames included in every Issue alongside nodeIds so the
  UI can display them without an extra round-trip
- suggestion-item uses flex-wrap so the layers panel expands
  below the message row

Monte Carlo : 500 simulations avec corrélations, fat tails et mean reversion

- Nouveau moteur MC dans model.js : rendements corrélés (Cholesky 4×4),
  queues épaisses (Student-t df=5), retour à la moyenne (AR(1) φ=-0.15)
- Second graphique dans App.jsx : bandes de confiance P10/P25/P50/P75/P90
  pour patrimoine et revenu mensuel réel
- Tooltip custom avec P10/P50/P90 + ligne déterministe en pointillés
- Taux de survie affiché en mode croquer capital
- Graphique revenu filtré à partir de ageObjectif (avant = déterministe)
- 31ms pour 500 simulations × 45 ans

commit.message:
Give name content terminals negative priority so keywords always win

GLOBAL_NAME_CONTENT gets priority -2 and LOCAL_NAME_CONTENT gets -1,
so keyword string terminals (priority 0) always win in lexing. This
improves error classification by producing specific keyword tokens
instead of name content blobs in error scenarios.

Also adds GlobalPositionDefinitionInLocalContext exception for when
'define the potential position' appears where only 'define the position'
is accepted, and improves ExtraWhitespace detection for spaces before
'and it does' and before open braces.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Linux musl build: patch ORT stacktrace.cc to remove execinfo.h dependency

The cmake flag onnxruntime_ENABLE_BACKTRACE=OFF does not prevent
stacktrace.cc from including execinfo.h directly. Patch the source
file to stub out backtrace calls on musl where execinfo.h is absent.

Address feedback that neko-gundan feels heavy for small tasks.

while agents can escalate via ESCALATION-001 if they detect hidden risk.

- Add modules/process-weight.md: ESCALATION-001 protocol definition
- Add docs/process-weight.md and .ja.md: full guide with examples
- Update install.sh: process-weight included in all modes automatically
- Update both READMEs: process weight section + documentation links

Three weights: Light (test+diff only), Standard (default), Strict (ensemble+ISV)
Safety protocols are never reduced regardless of weight.

commit.message:
Fix CI: SBOM needs --legacy-peer-deps, pipeline uses PRs for branch protection

- security-scan.yml: add --legacy-peer-deps to npm ci, --ignore-npm-errors
  to CycloneDX (React 19 peer dep mismatches cause npm ls errors)
- pin-pipeline.yml: create PR instead of pushing directly to main, since
  branch protection blocks github-actions[bot] direct pushes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Mission-aware agents, human name in prompts, doc audit

Agent prompts now carry the full mission context:
- Soul statement ("take care of your human, humanity, yourself")
- Mission (civilisation engine, thirteen product graphs, revenue model)
- Method (derivation over accumulation, social grammar, Code Graph)
- Trust model (start strict, earn autonomy)
- Human operator's name woven into every prompt

Each role prompt adds role-specific instructions on top of the
shared mission context. Agents know who their human is, what
they're building, and why.

Docs updated:
- CLAUDE.md: correct thirteen product layers with composition grammars
- VISION.md: full product table with revenue model, links to
  EventGraph specs (product-layers.md, derivation-method.md,
  compositions/)

Tests added:
- TestSystemPromptCarriesMission: every role has SOUL, MISSION, TRUST
- TestSystemPromptIncludesHumanName: human name flows through

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix remote snapshot: use newline delimiter instead of null bytes

Windows text-mode pipes can drop null bytes, causing find -printf with
\0 delimiters to return empty output. Switch to newline delimiters which
work reliably across platforms. Added raw stdout length to debug output.

commit.message:
feat: add JSONL count lines and read lines API

Add two new endpoints for JSONL file operations:
- POST /v1/jsonl/count: count total lines in a JSONL file
- POST /v1/jsonl/read: read specific lines from a JSONL file with start_line and count params

Includes service layer, handler, SDK interface and implementations (HTTP + local), and unit tests.

feat: wire AI narrative generator into monthly WhatsApp + email + auto-generate on Scout upload

- clients/notifications.py: auto-generate narrative for every new Scout snapshot
  via generate_narrative_for_snapshot() immediately after diagnostic received event.
  Saves to client_narratives table; model_used logged (claude-sonnet-4-6 or template-fallback).

- monthly_whatsapp_notifier.py: _get_latest_narrative_wa() pulls whatsapp_message from
  client_narratives (last 45d). If found, uses AI narrative instead of template —
  portal link appended if not already present. _log_result() shared helper for both paths.

- client_monthly_email.py: _get_latest_narrative_email() pulls email_summary from
  client_narratives (last 45d). Injected as personalised intro before structured
  ROI/interventions block. Falls back to generic intro if no narrative exists.

All three paths degrade gracefully — no failure if table empty or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set.

Fix pingpong: strict KB-only answers + role-aware responses + no hallucination

System prompt rewritten with 3 key fixes:
1. KB-only answers: never hallucinate (no movie plots for "minority report")
2. Role-aware responses: "buyer-only feature" instead of "not available"
3. No general knowledge: redirect non-platform questions to platform topics

Both smalltalk and explain categories updated with role context,
tone guidance, and strict grounding rules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct rdev mouse button mapping on Windows for PTT

On Windows, rdev reports XBUTTON1=1 and XBUTTON2=2 but JS
MouseEvent.button uses 3 (back) and 4 (forward). Add +2 offset
on Windows to align with the keybind store values. Also add
debug logging for the global mouse listener.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use intitle: operator in Google Books to improve title search relevance

Previously GB used a general full-text query (q=<title>), which matched
"langsam" anywhere in book records — descriptions, reviews, subject
metadata — and returned unrelated books like notebooks ahead of the
actual title being searched.

Switching to intitle:"<phrase>" scopes the search to title fields only:
  - "geh langsam"  → finds "Geh langsam, wenn du es eilig hast"   ✓
  - "geh"          → finds all books whose title contains "geh"    ✓
  - Single-word queries use intitle:<word> (no quotes needed)
  - Multi-word queries use intitle:"<phrase>" for ordered matching
  - Author queries still use inauthor:"<name>" as before

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add 3-step onboarding walkthrough to auto-capture scanner

Adapts the V1 scan onboarding pattern for the auto-capture flow:
- Step 1: Overview (point at title page, keep scanning, review)
- Step 2: How It Works (green overlay, hold steady, condition dots, manual shutter)
- Step 3: Scanning Tips (even lighting, flat pages, one per capture, dark surface)

Both /scan/auto and /scan/opencv get this automatically via shared AutoScanFlow.

commit.message:
feat: Add automatic migration generation from schema diff (#7)

* feat: Add automatic migration generation from schema diff

Implement TypeORM-style auto-generation that compares current database
state with Drizzle entity definitions and generates migrations automatically.

Core Features:
- Schema introspection for PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite
- Schema loading from Drizzle entity files (supports multi-directory)
- Schema diffing engine to detect tables, columns, indexes, and FK changes
- SQL generation with dialect-specific DDL statements
- Auto-generated up() and down() migration methods
- Fallback to blank template when no schema changes detected

New Components:
- SchemaIntrospector: Read current database schema metadata (412 lines)
- SchemaLoader: Parse Drizzle table definitions from TS files (228 lines)
- SchemaDiffer: Compare schemas and detect changes (227 lines)
- SqlGenerator: Generate dialect-specific DDL (360 lines)

Configuration:
- Extended MigrationConfig with schemaFiles and dbCredentials
- MigrationGenerator now async to support database introspection
- Backward compatible with existing blank migration workflow

Testing:
- Added 46 unit tests (schema-differ, sql-generator)
- Added 53 integration tests (PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite)
- All 99 tests passing with real database connections

Dependencies:
- Added drizzle-kit@^0.30.0 for schema parsing
- Added better-sqlite3@^11.0.0 for SQLite testing

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Resolve ESLint errors and warnings

- Fix case block lexical declaration error in generator.ts
- Remove unused imports (TableIndex, ForeignKey) from sql-generator.ts
- Prefix unused variable with underscore in schema-loader.ts
- Auto-fix prettier/prettier formatting issues

Reduced lint issues from 65 to 43 (0 errors, 43 warnings).
Remaining warnings are acceptable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
warnings for dynamic database result handling.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

- Format README.md and example documentation files

- Format .eslintrc.json

All files now pass prettier --check validation.
Tests verified: 46 unit tests passing.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Fix ROAS calculation - use LTV-based revenue

Change revenue attribution from event_date to install_date:
- Previously: counted revenue events occurring in period
- Now: counts ALL revenue from users who INSTALLED in period

This gives proper LTV-based ROAS instead of same-period-only revenue.

commit.message:
feat: Add screenshot optimization — scale + color reduction (up to 95% smaller)

New parameters `scale` (0.1-1.0) and `colors` (2/4/16/256) for aidex_screenshot.
Reduces file size dramatically for LLM context (e.g., 108 KB → 5 KB with scale=0.5, colors=2).
Cross-platform post-processing: Windows (System.Drawing), macOS (sips), Linux (ImageMagick).
Tool description includes iterative strategy for AI assistants to auto-optimize.

fix: security hardening and bug fixes from comprehensive review

- fix(tbx): prevent heredoc injection via printf in tbx-app and tbx-export-apps
- fix(sketchybar): whitelist APP names in media_control to prevent osascript injection
- fix(sketchybar): fix malformed awk printf in ram.sh (100-$5"%")
- fix(sketchybar): guard timer file against /tmp symlink attack
- fix(tmux): remove duplicate `l` keybinding conflict (refresh-client vs select-pane)
- fix(tmux): use tmux-256color instead of deprecated screen-256color
- fix(tmux): remove hardcoded reattach-to-user-namespace, use HOMEBREW_PREFIX
- fix(bash): define _cache_eval in 00-env.bash (used by 99-integrations.bash)
- fix(bash): replace chezmoi template literal {{ .chezmoi.os }} with runtime OSTYPE detection in 10-aliases.bash
- fix(chezmoi): restrict SSH config ignore to macOS only, Linux always gets .ssh/config applied
- fix(chezmoi): allow GPG setup on Linux when op CLI is available
- fix(windows): use scoop upgrade --all instead of scoop update --all
- fix(nvim): remove hardcoded mac-arm64 platform override from ai.lua (init.lua handles it)
- fix(nvim): rename <leader>gb to <leader>gL for git branches to fix conflict with git-blame.nvim
- fix(nvim): remove duplicate split keybindings (leader-/leader| duplicate leader-sh/leader-sv)
- fix(nvim): remove duplicate buffer close keybindings (leader-x/X duplicate leader-bd/bD)
- fix(nvim): remove duplicate goimport autocmd in go.lua (lsp_on_attach already handles it)
- fix(claude): reduce PermissionRequest hook timeout from 86400s to 30s
- fix(scripts): explicit path separator in update-claude-skills cp command

fix: rewrite TikTok extraction for JSON DDP format (VU 2026 spec)

TikTok changed their export format from .txt to .json (user_data.json).
Several section names also changed; both old and new names are tried.

- Switch DDP_CATEGORIES from TXT to JSON, known_files: [user_data.json]
- Add _load_user_data() to read user_data.json from zip
- Add _get() helper to navigate nested JSON with fallback key names
- Add activity_summary_to_df (ActivitySummaryMap: watch/comment/share counts)
- Add settings_to_df (SettingsMap: following/FYP keyword filters)
- Add watch_history_to_df (VideoList, tries "Video Browsing History"/"Watch History")
- Add favorite_videos_to_df (FavoriteVideoList)
- Add follower_to_df (FansList)
- Add following_to_df (Following, tries "Following List"/"Following")
- Add hashtag_to_df (HashtagList)
- Add like_list_to_df (ItemFavoriteList)
- Add searches_to_df (SearchList, tries "Search History"/"Searches")
- Add share_history_to_df (ShareHistoryList)
- Add comments_to_df (CommentsList)
- Remove Videos/Posts table (no longer in DDP per spec)
- Remove old .txt-based regex extractors
